actions.label: Skip beef once a week
actions.description: Replace one weekly beef meal with beans or lentils

assumptions.claim: Beef produces ~27kg CO2e per kg of food
assumptions.source: Poore & Nemecek (2018), Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers, Science, Vol 360

assumptions.claim: Legumes produce ~2kg CO2e per kg of food
assumptions.source: Poore & Nemecek (2018), Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers, Science, Vol 360

assumptions.claim: Beef requires ~15,400L of water per kg
assumptions.source: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010), The green, blue and grey water footprint of farm animals and animal products, Water Footprint Network

assumptions.claim: Legumes require ~900L of water per kg
assumptions.source: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010), The green, blue and grey water footprint of farm animals and animal products, Water Footprint Network

assumptions.claim: Average US household spends ~$18 more per month on beef vs bean equivalent meals
assumptions.source: USDA Economic Research Service (2023), Average Retail Food and Energy Prices

actions.label: Skip chicken once a week
actions.description: Replace one weekly chicken meal with tofu or lentils

assumptions.claim: Chicken produces ~6.9kg CO2e per kg of food
assumptions.source: Poore & Nemecek (2018), Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers, Science, Vol 360

assumptions.claim: Chicken requires ~4,300L of water per kg
assumptions.source: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010), The green, blue and grey water footprint of farm animals and animal products, Water Footprint Network

assumptions.claim: Tofu produces ~3kg CO2e per kg of food
assumptions.source: Poore & Nemecek (2018), Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers, Science, Vol 360

assumptions.claim: Average US retail chicken costs ~$2-4 more per meal than tofu or lentil equivalent
assumptions.source: USDA Economic Research Service (2023), Average Retail Food and Energy Prices

actions.label: Swap bananas for apples
actions.description: Replace imported bananas with domestic apples

assumptions.claim: Bananas produce ~0.86kg CO2e per kg including long-haul refrigerated shipping
assumptions.source: Poore & Nemecek (2018), Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers, Science, Vol 360

assumptions.claim: Domestic apples have significantly lower transport emissions than imported bananas
assumptions.source: Weber & Matthews (2008), Food-Miles and the Relative Climate Impacts of Food Choices, Environmental Science & Technology

assumptions.claim: Average US household wastes ~30% of fresh fruit purchased
assumptions.source: USDA Economic Research Service (2020), Household Food Security in the United States

assumptions.claim: Bananas require ~790L of water per kg vs ~700L for apples
assumptions.source: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010), The green, blue and grey water footprint of crops and crop products, Water Footprint Network

actions.label: Swap olive oil for vegetable oil
actions.description: Use domestic vegetable oil instead of imported olive oil

assumptions.claim: Olive oil averages $12-15 per liter at US retail vs $3-4 for vegetable oil
assumptions.source: USDA Economic Research Service (2023), Average Retail Food and Energy Prices

assumptions.claim: Olive oil produces ~6kg CO2e per kg vs ~3.5kg for soybean oil
assumptions.source: Poore & Nemecek (2018), Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers, Science, Vol 360

assumptions.claim: Olive oil is entirely import-dependent for US consumers, primarily from Spain and Italy
assumptions.source: USDA Foreign Agricultural Service (2023), Oilseeds: World Markets and Trade

actions.label: Diversify away from white rice
actions.description: Shift some rice meals to oats or pasta to reduce single-grain dependence

assumptions.claim: Rice produces ~4kg CO2e per kg due to methane emissions from paddy fields
assumptions.source: Poore & Nemecek (2018), Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers, Science, Vol 360

assumptions.claim: Rice requires ~1,670L of water per kg vs ~1,400L for oats
assumptions.source: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010), The green, blue and grey water footprint of crops and crop products, Water Footprint Network

assumptions.claim: Over 90% of US rice consumption is import-dependent or regionally concentrated
assumptions.source: USDA Economic Research Service (2023), Rice Outlook

actions.label: Reduce egg reliance during avian flu pressure
actions.description: Mix in tofu or beans for some egg-based meals

assumptions.claim: Egg prices increased 49% year-over-year during 2022-2023 avian flu outbreak
assumptions.source: US Bureau of Labor Statistics (2023), Consumer Price Index, Eggs

assumptions.claim: Eggs produce ~4.5kg CO2e per kg
assumptions.source: Poore & Nemecek (2018), Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers, Science, Vol 360

assumptions.claim: Eggs require ~3,300L of water per kg
assumptions.source: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010), The green, blue and grey water footprint of farm animals and animal products, Water Footprint Network

actions.label: Shift from fresh to canned tomatoes
actions.description: Use canned tomatoes for cooked dishes instead of fresh imported tomatoes

assumptions.claim: Over 60% of fresh tomatoes consumed in the US are imported from Mexico
assumptions.source: USDA Foreign Agricultural Service (2023), Vegetables and Pulses Outlook

assumptions.claim: Fresh tomatoes have a household waste rate of ~25% vs near-zero for canned
assumptions.source: ReFED (2021), Insights Engine, Food Waste Data

assumptions.claim: Canned tomatoes are predominantly domestically produced in California
assumptions.source: USDA National Agricultural Statistics Service (2023), Vegetables Summary

actions.label: Reduce avocado purchases during disruption
actions.description: Cut avocado frequency in half during high trade or shipping risk periods

assumptions.claim: Each avocado requires approximately 320L of water to produce
assumptions.source: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010), The green, blue and grey water footprint of crops and crop products, Water Footprint Network

assumptions.claim: Over 80% of US avocados are imported from Mexico
assumptions.source: USDA Foreign Agricultural Service (2023), Tropical Fruits: World Markets and Trade

assumptions.claim: Avocados have one of the highest household spoilage rates among fresh produce at ~35%
assumptions.source: ReFED (2021), Insights Engine, Food Waste Data

assumptions.claim: Avocados produce ~2.5kg CO2e per kg including transport
assumptions.source: Poore & Nemecek (2018), Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers, Science, Vol 360

actions.label: Stock up on shelf-stable proteins
actions.description: Buy ahead on dried beans, lentils, canned tuna during demand surge signals

assumptions.claim: Retail food prices spike 15-30% during supply disruption events
assumptions.source: USDA Economic Research Service (2023), Food Price Outlook

assumptions.claim: Dried legumes have a shelf life of 1-2 years when stored correctly
assumptions.source: USDA Food Safety and Inspection Service (2023), Shelf-Stable Food Safety

assumptions.claim: Shelf-stable proteins have near-zero household food waste rates
assumptions.source: ReFED (2021), Insights Engine, Food Waste Data

actions.label: Reduce premium coffee brand dependence
actions.description: Switch to lower-cost domestic blends during import pressure

assumptions.claim: Coffee production requires ~140L of water per cup or ~18,900L per kg
assumptions.source: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010), The green, blue and grey water footprint of crops and crop products, Water Footprint Network

assumptions.claim: Coffee produces ~17kg CO2e per kg including processing and shipping
assumptions.source: Poore & Nemecek (2018), Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers, Science, Vol 360

assumptions.claim: 100% of coffee consumed in the US is imported
assumptions.source: USDA Foreign Agricultural Service (2023), Coffee: World Markets and Trade

assumptions.claim: Premium single-origin coffee costs 2-3x commodity blends at US retail
assumptions.source: USDA Economic Research Service (2023), Average Retail Food and Energy Prices

scaling.note: Scale by multiplying per_household values by the scale multiplier. Campus assumes 1,250 households of 4 people totaling 5,000 people.

co2_kg_per_car_year.source: US EPA (2023), Greenhouse Gas Emissions from a Typical Passenger Vehicle

water_liters_per_person_day.source: USGS (2023), Water Questions and Answers, US per capita water use

food_waste_kg_per_household_year.source: ReFED (2021), Insights Engine, US household food waste average
